stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Lichens, organisms formed by a partnership between fungi and algae, grow in many climates, including tropical and polar regions.
  • Lichen diversity has been poorly documented in the cold, windswept islands of the South Atlantic.
  • Botanist Tomas Halvorsen led a study to determine how many lichen species inhabit the Falkland Islands.
  • Halvorsen and his team sampled lichens at coastal and inland sites over twelve months.
  • The team recorded 156 lichen species at the surveyed sites.
stem: The student wants to present the study and its findings. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Lichens grow in many environments, and 156 lichen species inhabit the Falkland Islands.
Tomas Halvorsen and his team wondered how many lichen species inhabit the Falkland Islands, so they conducted a study by sampling at coastal and inland sites.
A study led by Tomas Halvorsen recorded a total of 156 lichen species at sampled sites in the Falkland Islands.
The Falkland Islands, located in the South Atlantic, are home to 156 lichen species.

rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. This choice most effectively presents the study and its findings. It opens with the study and names its lead researcher, then reports the finding: 156 lichen species were recorded at sampled sites in the Falkland Islands.

Choice A is incorrect. The sentence doesn’t mention that there was a study at all, so it fails to present the study. Choice B is incorrect. The sentence presents the aim and methodology of the study, but it omits the findings. Choice D is incorrect. The sentence reports a fact about the islands but doesn’t mention that the figure came from a study.
